My Latest Take

South Africa vs Israel: Why We Shouldn't Be Involved

We barged into the ICJ screaming "genocide" while our own house is on fire. Youth unemployment, crime, corruption — and Ramaphosa wants to play global saviour? This isn't leadership. It's an ego trip. Here's my honest take.

What I'm Thinking About

📉

Youth Unemployment

My Take • March 6, 2026

60% youth unemployment isn't a crisis — it's a scam. A system built to fail young people, then blame them for it. I'm tired of watching it happen.

→ 🚨

Crime & Safety

My Take • March 5, 2026

27,000 murders in 2024. We haven't solved crime — we've stopped believing we can. And criminals know it. Here's my take on what's really going on.

→ ⚖️

Corruption

My Take • March 4, 2026

We call it corruption. It's not — it's theft. And the system isn't broken. It's working exactly as designed. Here's why nothing ever gets fixed.

Cost of Living

My Take • March 3, 2026

Bread. Milk. Eggs. Electricity. Every receipt is proof of a system failing us. South Africans aren't living anymore — we're surviving.

→ 🌊

Water Crisis

My Take • March 2, 2026

You wake up. No water. This isn't a drought — it's mismanagement. South Africa isn't running out of water. We're running out of accountability.

I'm Mr EchoPoint. I share my takes on South African news and current events. Not everyone will agree, and that's fine—I'm here to offer one perspective on what's happening and why it matters.

This is commentary. This is analysis. This is one voice in the conversation. I believe honest dialogue about real issues is how we move forward together.
